commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor-provided real behavior proof, which automation cannot supply for their live Zen-only account setup.; Cleared: The diff changes OpenCode Go provider fetching/snapshot logic, stubbed tests, and release notes; it does not add dependencies, workflows, credentials handling, or a new supply-chain surface. (sha=59e5fca0a5836f376f71e1f03bdd75365a32894e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes OpenCode Go fetching and snapshots so Zen-only accounts can return a balance-only usage snapshot, adds a slow-balance regression test, and updates the changelog.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main throws `OpenCode Go parse error: Missing usage fields.` when the Go page lacks subscription usage fields, and the linked report describes the Zen-only account state. I did not run a live provider probe because AGENTS.md requires explicit permission for real account or browser-cookie access.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor-provided real behavior proof, which automation cannot supply for their live Zen-only account setup.

target.title: fix(opencode): show Zen balance for Go accounts without subscription usage
target.body:
## Summary
- allow OpenCode Go accounts without Go subscription usage fields to fall back to the existing Zen balance fetch
- render Zen-only accounts as balance-only snapshots instead of false 0% rate windows
- wait for the balance request as required fallback data while keeping the 250 ms grace only for optional enrichment
- cancel the parallel balance task on unrelated failures and use synchronized request recording in concurrent tests
- keep the existing parse error when neither Go subscription usage nor Zen balance is available

Fixes #1476

## Tests
- `swift test --filter OpenCodeGoUsageFetcherErrorTests --skip-update` (15 tests passed)
- `make check` (SwiftFormat clean, SwiftLint 0 violations, packaging/release-path checks passed)
- `git diff --check`
- autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings

## Proof and risk
- the regression delays the Zen balance response by 400 ms, beyond the optional 250 ms enrichment grace, and verifies a balance-only snapshot still renders
- no live account or browser-cookie probe was run; repository policy requires explicit permission for live provider probes that may access browser or Keychain state
- provider auth, cookie normalization, endpoints, and storage are unchanged
target.author: kiranmagic7

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
